title: USD Stage Population Spec Coverage Ledger

normative_source.rule: The pinned stage_population specification is normative. This ledger decomposes the parts covered by the first stage-population skill and names dependencies or deferrals for the rest.

status_values.implemented: In scope for this skill contract and covered by goldens.
status_values.provider_dependent: Owned by the stage-population contract but semantically limited by the NamespaceSource capability level.
status_values.deferred: Specified but intentionally not implemented in the first stage-population contract.
status_values.out_of_scope: Belongs to another subsystem or future skill.

coverage_rules: Stage population consumes a NamespaceSource so it can be tested now with a single-layer source and later with composed sources.
A generated target must not claim full composition, value resolution, payload loading, or schema fallback support unless the consumed NamespaceSource declares that capability.
Stage population may preserve schema-defined properties supplied by a schema-aware NamespaceSource, but must not compute prim definitions locally.
Population and query semantics must use target-native paths, tokens, and field values; canonical JSON is only a conformance boundary.

obligations: Create a root scene object at /.
Populate reachable child prim paths from source child names.
Expose ordered stage traversal for populated prims.
Include requested paths and their ancestors.
Exclude unrelated branches.
Do not let a mask force descendants under inactive ancestors into the populated stage.
Apply active=true fallback when no active opinion exists.
Do not populate inactive prims or their descendants in normal traversal.
Keep active=false as layer data in the document model; pruning belongs only to population.
For a single-layer source, reorder source primChildren with that spec's primOrder.
For a single-layer source, sort source properties by path element order and apply that spec's propertyOrder.
For composed sources that declare composed ordering, preserve provider-supplied child/property order while still applying population masks and active pruning.
For schema-aware sources, preserve schema-defined property names supplied by the provider.
Expose Active, Loaded, Model Hierarchy, Defined, Abstract, and Instance query flags where the provider has sufficient opinions.
Use fallback policy for missing active, instanceable, kind, and specifier fields.
Do not evaluate non-portable model hierarchy aliases.
Stage population may expose source-visible authored fields for structural queries.
Any stage property API that returns resolved values must call the usd-value-resolution provider instead of reimplementing value resolution locally.

A populated stage can only claim resolved values when its consumed NamespaceSource supplies the opinion stacks, retiming, schema fallback context, and clip context required by the value-resolution contract.

spec_refs: stage_population/README.md:176 matching criteria may not be sufficient with population masks
obligations: Treat a prim as a supported instance only when it is marked instanceable and supported composition-arc provenance is available.
Assign shared representation identities for matching supported composition-arc provenance when population-mask context is compatible.
Do not share otherwise matching instanceable prims when the population mask affects their subtrees differently.
Consume instance-filtered descendant inputs from the composed namespace source so local descendant opinions under an instanceable prim are excluded for supported non-local composition arc families.
Include portable group and assembly prims in the model hierarchy and let them continue hierarchy traversal.
Include portable component prims but do not include descendants of those components in the model hierarchy.
Exclude subcomponent, empty, and unknown kind values and terminate model hierarchy continuity through them.
Leave custom aliases and validation diagnostics implementation-dependent.

items.deferred_reason: The current work defines stage population contracts only. C API backend compatibility should consume this skill later.
